name: Agricultural Engineering
description: Agricultural Engineering applies engineering principles to agricultural production, processing, and environmental management. The curriculum covers farm machinery, irrigation, food processing, renewable energy, and precision farming technologies.

subjects.name: Farm Machinery
subjects.description: Study of agricultural machinery used for tillage, sowing, planting, inter-cultivation, harvesting, and threshing. Covers machine design, power requirements, and operational efficiency.
learning_outcomes: Classify farm machinery based on agricultural operations
Calculate power requirements for tillage and harvesting equipment
Evaluate the performance and efficiency of farm machines
Select appropriate machinery for different farming operations
subjects.industry_relevance: Farm mechanization is critical for improving agricultural productivity and reducing labor dependency in farming.

subjects.name: Soil & Water Conservation
subjects.description: Principles of soil erosion, water conservation techniques, and watershed management. Covers erosion control structures, contour farming, terracing, and soil moisture conservation methods.
learning_outcomes: Identify types and causes of soil erosion
Design soil conservation structures and practices
Plan water conservation strategies for agricultural lands
Evaluate watershed management techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Soil and water conservation is vital for sustainable agriculture, especially in regions facing water scarcity and land degradation.

subjects.name: Irrigation Engineering
subjects.description: Design and management of irrigation systems including surface, sprinkler, and drip irrigation methods. Covers water conveyance, distribution networks, pump selection, and irrigation scheduling.
learning_outcomes: Design surface, sprinkler, and drip irrigation systems
Calculate irrigation water requirements and scheduling
Select pumps and distribution systems for irrigation
Evaluate irrigation system performance and uniformity
subjects.industry_relevance: Efficient irrigation is essential for food security and water conservation, with smart irrigation systems gaining rapid adoption.

subjects.name: Agricultural Structures
subjects.description: Design and construction of agricultural buildings including farm houses, animal shelters, storage structures, greenhouses, and silos. Covers environmental control, structural loads, and building materials.
learning_outcomes: Design farm structures for storage and animal housing
Analyze structural loads on agricultural buildings
Design greenhouse structures with environmental control
Evaluate storage requirements for grains and perishables
subjects.industry_relevance: Proper agricultural structures reduce post-harvest losses and improve farm productivity through controlled environments.

subjects.name: Food Process Engineering
subjects.description: Engineering principles applied to food processing including heat and mass transfer, drying, evaporation, freezing, and packaging. Covers food quality, preservation techniques, and process equipment design.
learning_outcomes: Apply heat and mass transfer principles to food processing
Design drying, evaporation, and freezing systems for food
Evaluate food preservation techniques and packaging methods
Analyze food quality parameters during processing
subjects.industry_relevance: Food processing engineering is essential for reducing food waste and adding value to agricultural produce.

subjects.name: Post Harvest Technology
subjects.description: Study of post-harvest handling, storage, and processing of agricultural produce including grains, fruits, and vegetables. Covers drying, cleaning, grading, packaging, and cold chain management.
learning_outcomes: Design post-harvest handling systems for different crops
Implement drying and storage techniques to reduce losses
Design grading and sorting systems for agricultural produce
Manage cold chain logistics for perishable products
subjects.industry_relevance: Post-harvest technology reduces food losses and adds value to agricultural produce, critical for food security.

subjects.name: Agricultural Extension
subjects.description: Principles and methods of agricultural extension including technology transfer, rural development, communication strategies, and farmer training programs. Covers extension program planning and evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Plan and implement agricultural extension programs
Apply communication strategies for technology transfer
Evaluate the impact of extension services on farming communities
Develop training materials for farmer education
subjects.industry_relevance: Agricultural extension bridges the gap between research and farming practice, driving technology adoption in rural areas.

subjects.name: Watershed Management
subjects.description: Integrated watershed management including hydrological analysis, land use planning, soil conservation, water harvesting, and community participation. Covers remote sensing and GIS applications in watershed management.
learning_outcomes: Conduct hydrological analysis for watershed planning
Design water harvesting structures for rural areas
Apply GIS and remote sensing for watershed assessment
Develop community-based watershed management plans
subjects.industry_relevance: Watershed management is critical for sustainable water resources, flood control, and rural livelihood improvement.

subjects.name: Renewable Energy in Agriculture
subjects.description: Application of renewable energy sources in agriculture including solar, wind, biomass, and biogas systems. Covers solar pumps, biomass gasifiers, biogas plants, and energy-efficient agricultural practices.
learning_outcomes: Design solar photovoltaic systems for agricultural applications
Develop biogas plants for farm energy needs
Evaluate wind and biomass energy potential for rural areas
Implement energy-efficient practices in agricultural operations
subjects.industry_relevance: Renewable energy reduces farm operating costs and carbon footprint, aligning with global sustainability goals.

subjects.name: Precision Farming
subjects.description: Technology-driven farming approach using GPS, GIS, remote sensing, variable rate technology, and IoT sensors for optimized crop management. Covers yield monitoring, soil mapping, and site-specific input application.
learning_outcomes: Apply GPS and GIS technologies for precision agriculture
Implement variable rate technology for fertilizer and pesticide application
Analyze remote sensing data for crop health monitoring
Design IoT-based monitoring systems for smart farming
subjects.industry_relevance: Precision farming increases crop yields while reducing input costs and environmental impact, driving agri-tech innovation.

subjects.name: Dairy Engineering
subjects.description: Engineering principles applied to dairy processing including milk reception, pasteurization, homogenization, separation, and packaging. Covers dairy plant design, equipment, and quality control.
learning_outcomes: Design dairy processing equipment and plant layouts
Apply heat transfer principles to pasteurization and sterilization
Evaluate milk quality parameters and testing methods
Design packaging systems for dairy products
subjects.industry_relevance: The dairy industry requires specialized engineering for processing, preservation, and quality assurance of milk products.

subjects.name: Farm Power & Tractors
subjects.description: Study of farm power sources including tractors, engines, and electric motors used in agriculture. Covers tractor systems, power transmission, hydraulic systems, and tractor-implement matching.
learning_outcomes: Analyze tractor power systems and performance characteristics
Match tractors with appropriate implements for field operations
Design tractor hydraulic systems for implement control
Evaluate tractor fuel efficiency and power requirements
subjects.industry_relevance: Tractors remain the primary power source in agriculture, with ongoing advancements in precision and autonomous operation.

subjects.name: Seed Technology
subjects.description: Seed production, processing, testing, and storage technologies including seed certification, treatment, and quality control. Covers seed physiology, genetic purity, and seed enhancement techniques.
learning_outcomes: Apply seed production and certification procedures
Design seed processing and treatment systems
Evaluate seed quality through germination and vigor tests
Implement seed storage techniques for long-term viability
subjects.industry_relevance: Quality seed production is fundamental to agricultural productivity and food security worldwide.

subjects.name: Crop Production
subjects.description: Scientific principles of crop production including plant physiology, soil science, nutrient management, and pest control. Covers major field crops, cropping systems, and sustainable agricultural practices.
learning_outcomes: Identify major field crops and their growing requirements
Apply soil nutrient management and fertilization techniques
Design cropping systems for sustainable production
Implement integrated pest management strategies
subjects.industry_relevance: Crop production knowledge is fundamental to all agricultural engineering roles and farm management.

subjects.name: Agricultural Waste Management
subjects.description: Management and utilization of agricultural waste including crop residues, animal manure, and agro-industrial waste. Covers composting, biogas production, waste-to-energy conversion, and environmental impact assessment.
learning_outcomes: Design waste management systems for agricultural operations
Develop biogas and composting systems for organic waste
Evaluate environmental impacts of agricultural waste
Implement waste-to-energy conversion technologies
subjects.industry_relevance: Agricultural waste management addresses environmental pollution while creating value from waste through energy and compost production.
